name: mb_dstore
m5_band: INFO regression: STORES warm the D-cache (read-allocate); a later reg-indirect load to a stored line HITS (no dmiss). Checks D-cache STATE consistency, not abs-cyc (the disp stores run the cycle-APPROXIMATE slow FSM, so abs-cyc is dominated by slow-path store cost — the M4 honest caveat, not a state bug).
note: Regression for finding [med] D-cache state. p5_mem runs l1_access for stores too. M5 runs dc_access on slow-path stores+loads so the D-cache miss SEQUENCE matches the oracle: 199/200 reg-indirect loads HIT because the preceding stores warmed their lines. abs-cyc is NOT the check here (slow-path disp-store timing is approximate by design).
